name: BeiDou

overview.value: BeiDou is a global satellite navigation system operated by the China National Space Administration. The system has evolved through three generations: BeiDou-1, a regional experimental system decommissioned in December 2012; BeiDou-2, a regional system covering the Asia-Pacific from December 2012; and BeiDou-3, which reached full global coverage in July 2020. BeiDou-3 uses a mixed constellation of satellites in medium Earth orbit, inclined geosynchronous orbit, and geostationary orbit, offering a free civilian positioning service alongside an encrypted higher-accuracy service for authorized users.

orbit.value: Mixed MEO/IGSO/GEO: 24 MEO + 3 IGSO + 3 GEO (BeiDou-3 nominal constellation)

service_type.value: Free public civilian service (about 3.6 m global accuracy) and an encrypted military/authorized service (about 10 cm accuracy)

notes: Added 2026-07-13 on Florian's instruction, for competitive context against commercial connectivity/IoT constellations. Government-owned system: orbits deliberately set to null (mixed MEO/IGSO/GEO would break the MCC 3D LEO view) and no celestrak fields filled. The official constellation status source named in the task (csno-tarc.cn) returned a TLS hostname mismatch and en.beidou.gov.cn refused the connection outright; both were unfetchable as anticipated, so all facts are sourced to Wikipedia (unscored) instead. Wikipedia's List of BeiDou satellites infobox itself is dated 'as of December 2023' for its launch totals; that is the most recent figure the fetched page states, so more recent 2024-2026 launches may not be reflected in sats_launched_total/sats_active_claimed/latest_launch_date. Weekly maintenance 2026-07-13: the page's launch table lists a more recent row, BeiDou-3 M27 on 2024-09-19, so latest_launch_date was upgraded to that date. sats_launched_total/sats_active_claimed were left at 62/44: two separate fetch passes of the same page returned inconsistent total-row counts (62 vs 60), so no reliable refreshed total could be confirmed this run; the discrepancy is noted here rather than guessed at.

generations.name: BeiDou-1
generations.text: Regional experimental system with three operational satellites, decommissioned December 2012.

generations.name: BeiDou-2
generations.text: Regional system of 16 satellites providing Asia-Pacific services from December 2012, with positioning accuracy to within 25 metres during trials.

generations.name: BeiDou-3
generations.text: Global system providing millimeter-level accuracy with post-processing.

events.headline: BeiDou-1A, the first BeiDou satellite, launches
BeiDou-2 begins regional Asia-Pacific navigation service
Final BeiDou-3 satellite launches, completing the constellation
BeiDou-3 declared fully operational with global coverage
Most recent BeiDou satellite launch in the current catalog
